steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The graph shown is $y=f(x)+2$, so graph each answer choice shifted up by $2$ and compare directly to the displayed curve. In Desmos, choice B becomes $y=-3^x+5$, which has the correct horizontal asymptote and point placement.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $f(x)=-3^{x}+1$: asymptote at $y = 1$, not 3. ✗
  • Choice B — $f(x)=-3^{x}+3$: asymptote at $y = 3$. Check: $f(1) = -3 + 3 = 0$, so $f(1)+2 = 2$ — matches the graph. ✓
  • Choice C — $f(x)=-3^{x}+4$: asymptote at $y = 4$, not 3. ✗
  • Choice D — $f(x)=-3^{x}+5$: asymptote at $y = 5$, not 3. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
